title: Abstract operations (ToNumber, ToString)
body:
## Definition

**Abstract operations** like **ToNumber** and **ToString** are spec-defined algorithms that convert values for operators, comparisons, and built-in functions.

## Why it exists

Explains `+`, `==`, `Number()`, and `parseInt()` behavior consistently under [type coercion](concept:type-coercion).

## How it works

```js
Number("42"); // ToNumber â†’ 42
String(42); // ToString â†’ "42"
parseInt("42px", 10); // parses leading digits
+"42"; // unary + invokes ToNumber
```

`ToPrimitive` chooses number vs string hint for objects before coercion.

## Common confusion

`Number(null)` is 0, `Number(undefined)` is NaN. `parseInt` truncates; `Number` parses full string or NaN.

## In practice

Interview fodder for `[] + {}` and `==` puzzles â€” map answers to spec conversions, then recommend `===` and explicit parsing.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[Abstract operations](concept:abstract-operations)** like **[ToNumber](concept:abstract-operations)** and **[ToString](concept:abstract-operations)** are spec-defined algorithms that convert values for operators, comparisons, and built-in functions.

## Why it exists

Explains `+`, [==](concept:loose-equality), [Number()](concept:abstract-operations), and [parseInt()](concept:abstract-operations) behavior consistently under [type coercion](concept:type-coercion).

## How it works

```js
Number("42"); // ToNumber â†’ 42
String(42); // ToString â†’ "42"
parseInt("42px", 10); // parses leading digits
+"42"; // unary + invokes ToNumber
```

`ToPrimitive` chooses number vs string hint for objects before coercion.

## Common confusion

`Number(null)` is 0, `Number(undefined)` is [NaN](concept:nan). `parseInt` truncates; `Number` parses full string or [NaN](concept:nan).

## In practice

Interview fodder for `[] + {}` and [==](concept:loose-equality) puzzles â€” [map](concept:array-map-filter-reduce) answers to spec conversions, then recommend [===](concept:strict-equality) and explicit parsing.
